session_summary: Marcus reviews the canonical sourced derivation (derivation.qmd/html) and drives a wholesale restructuring for an outside theoretical-statistician reader: he demands a high-level roadmap, a proof sketch, and motivating prose around the bare algebra, and rejects house jargon ('ledge'/'numerical witness'/'consume'). His role is editorial and standard-setting plus quality control of fair-use handling of verbatim quotes; he repeatedly redirects to commit first and decides what stays vs. cuts.

coded_turns.note: Opens the session by directing the AI to read the rendered derivation; plain operational kickoff.

coded_turns.quote: I need more information in this:   A couple of careful design points stand out
coded_turns.note: Flags that the RIV/Q-bar disambiguation callouts are under-explained and need more content.
Probes the AI to articulate the actual fix for the (A)+(C) cancellation, not just describe behavior.

coded_turns.quote: What would a skeptical reviewer want?
coded_turns.note: Reframes the agenda around referee scrutiny, eliciting the documentation-gap (proof vs pointer) diagnosis.
Picks the fix-the-keystone-now option over running the reviewer persona pass.
Operational request to re-render the derivation document after the lemma edit.

coded_turns.quote: I don't think these [cite], [mc], etc. bracket things in text are needed any more now that we have the visibility in the margins. Correct?
coded_turns.note: Calls for removing in-text provenance brackets now that margin flags carry it; declutters body.

coded_turns.quote: still is not outside reader friendly. First there are some words that a theoretical statistician would never use like "ledge" or "numerical witness"
coded_turns.note: Rejects house jargon and demands an intro roadmap, proof sketch, and motivating prose around the algebra.
coded_turns.artifact_hint: T-03 banned vocabulary / outside-reader feedback; derivation restructure

coded_turns.quote: Should we enter plan mode?. Margin-provenance is good. Agree with you on applications.
coded_turns.note: Endorses plan mode for the reorg and ratifies margin-provenance and applications-as-pointers.

coded_turns.quote: Before you do that. let's commit
coded_turns.note: Interrupts to commit current state before the large reorg begins; checkpoint discipline.

coded_turns.quote: Before you do that. let's commit
coded_turns.note: Repeats the commit-first instruction (resend); enforce checkpoint before reorganization.

coded_turns.quote: Do we ever relate any of our terms to the derivations of the AICcd?
coded_turns.note: Pushes to connect the new terms to Cavanaugh-Shumway AICcd lineage; positioning check.

coded_turns.quote: consume is a weird word. but its seems fine to continue
coded_turns.note: Flags 'consume' as off-register (later banned) while approving continuation of the rewrite.

coded_turns.quote: a human reader woudl like more text in the 3.1. The's too muhcin paragraph in 3.2..kinda expand it vertically. Is this necessary
coded_turns.note: Demands more prose in 3.1, splits the dense 3.2 paragraph, and questions a load-bearing bold sentence.

coded_turns.quote: we can make a note that these verbatim will be deleted after the blinded peer review process terminated. That said, we need more of the surrounding words around the verbatim text
coded_turns.note: Sets fair-use handling rule for verbatim quotes: delete-after-review note plus surrounding context.

ai_side.evidence: If you meant #2 ... I can pull the normal/exponential cancellation out of the MI-IC derivation and write it into A4 (or a collapsible appendix block)
ai_side.note: Offered to make A4 self-contained vs. leave it as a pointer.

ai_side.evidence: Two ways forward ... 1. Run `/review reviewer1` ... 2. Fix the top item now ... Which do you want?
ai_side.note: Laid out reviewer-persona pass vs. immediate fix as a choice.

ai_side.evidence: If that architecture matches your mental model, I'll start by drafting ... Want me to go — or adjust the skeleton first?
ai_side.note: Put the restructuring skeleton up for approval before drafting.

ai_side.evidence: is this the register you want for an outside theoretical statistician? ... Too much structure for an intro, or right?
ai_side.note: Asked the human to ratify voice/level and intro structure.

ai_side.evidence: This was a large rewrite (+292/−189). Want me to commit it? I'd propose, on `main` (your working branch)
ai_side.note: Surfaced the commit decision with a proposed message.
